summary: The AI Customer Support Rollout plan has major gaps between leadership expectations (autonomous responses) and legal compliance requirements (mandatory human review of all outbound AI content). There is no allocation of staff or time in the project plan to perform this review, and success criteria are extremely vague, increasing the likelihood of launch delays or compliance breaches.
topBlockers: Conflict between leadership's expectation of fully autonomous AI and legal's requirement for manual human review.
No resource allocation or hiring plan for review agents in the current project schedule.
Vague, unquantifiable success metrics ('improved customer experience') rather than specific operational KPIs.

risks.title: Autonomous AI vs Legal compliance conflict

evidence.excerpt: Leadership expects autonomous responses. Legal requires human review before customer communication.

risks.implementationImpact: The project cannot go live in fully autonomous mode. Attempting to bypass legal will result in regulatory non-compliance, while enforcing human review will bottleneck the support queue unless staffed.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Implement a tiered rollout: start with 'co-pilot' mode (human-in-the-loop review) for the first 30 days, then move to autonomous responses for low-risk, verified intents.
risks.suggestedBlockerReason: AI rollout lacks legal compliance approval.
risks.confidenceReason: Contradiction explicitly stated between leadership and legal.

risks.title: Unallocated human review staffing

evidence.excerpt: The implementation schedule does not include review staffing.

risks.implementationImpact: Support agents will be overwhelmed by reviewing AI drafts on top of their normal duties, leading to severe SLA breaches and long ticket resolution queues.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Calculate required reviewer headcount based on ticket volume. Reallocate 3 senior agents to the review queue or adjust the launch timeline to allow hiring.
risks.suggestedBlockerReason: No resources allocated for review queue.

risks.title: Undefined quantitative success metrics

evidence.excerpt: Success metrics are described only as 'improved customer experience.'

risks.implementationImpact: Inability to demonstrate project ROI or model efficacy. Without hard KPIs, success will be subjective and vulnerable to random negative feedback.
risks.requiredClarificationOrAction: Define specific success metrics: target CSAT of 4.2+, AI resolution rate of 30%, and average response time under 15 minutes.

stakeholders.name: Corporate Leadership
conflicts: Expects autonomous responses without human delay
stakeholders.authority: Owns budget approval and final timeline decisions

stakeholders.name: Legal & Compliance Team
goals: Eliminate risk of AI hallucinations and brand damage
Ensure data privacy regulations are strictly followed
conflicts: Demands manual review of every message before sending
stakeholders.authority: Can halt the project launch due to compliance liability

dependencies.name: Review Queue Interface
dependencies.description: Custom ticketing interface allowing agents to edit and approve AI draft replies.

dependencies.name: AI Output Guardrails Layer
dependencies.description: Integration of moderation APIs and intent-filtering rules to block off-topic content.

decisions.decision: Approve transition criteria from Human-in-the-loop to Autonomous operation
decisions.consequence: Failure to define this will trap the system in an expensive, semi-automated state forever.

validationQuestions.question: Does Legal require all customer data sent to the AI model to be anonymized first?
What is the baseline customer satisfaction (CSAT) score we are measuring against?

rolloutRecommendations.action: Configure CRM draft-generation queue for testing AI outputs internally.
Launch pilot phase with 3 dedicated reviewer agents validating drafts before dispatch.
Evaluate AI performance and safety logs; transition low-risk intents to autonomous release.
